ddls:
CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the node */,
  "cpu_type" TEXT /* Type of CPU used in the node */,
  "cpu_cores" INTEGER /* Number of CPU cores in the node */,
  "memory" INTEGER /* Total memory available in the node */,
  "storage" TEXT /* Type of storage used in the node (e.g., NVMe, HDD) */,
  "gpu_type" TEXT /* Type of GPU used in the node (e.g., NVIDIA A100, AMD Radeon) */,
  "gpu_count" INTEGER /* Number of GPUs in the node */,
  "gpu_memory" INTEGER /* Total memory available in the GPUs */,
  "network_type" TEXT /* Type of network used (e.g., InfiniBand, Ethernet) */,
  "network_speed" TEXT /* Speed of the network (e.g., 100 Gbps) */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last maintenance performed on the node */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the node (e.g., active, maintenance, offline) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node the metric belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was collected */,
  "cpu_utilization" REAL /* CPU utilization of the node */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the node */,
  "nvme_from_ceph" REAL /* NVMe storage usage from Ceph */,
  "nvme_from_transfer" REAL /* NVMe storage usage from transfer */,
  "nvme_total_util" REAL /* Total NVMe storage utilization */,
  "goodput" REAL /* Goodput of the node */,
  "packet_losses" REAL /* Packet losses of the node */,
  "network_throughput" REAL /* Network throughput of the node */,
  "gpu_utilization" REAL /* GPU utilization of the node */,
  "gpu_memory_usage" REAL /* GPU memory usage of the node */,
  "power_usage" REAL /* Power consumption of the node */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the node */,
  "io_latency" REAL /* Input/output latency */,
  "network_latency" REAL /* Network latency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or research group the user belongs to */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation or institution of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for contact purposes */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the job */,
  "job_name" TEXT /* Name of the job */,
  "job_status" TEXT /* Status of the job (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node the job is running on */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job started */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the job ended */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the job */,
  "resources_requested" TEXT /* Resources requested by the job (e.g., CPU cores, memory, GPUs) */,
  "resources_allocated" TEXT /* Resources allocated to the job */,
  "estimated_runtime" REAL /* Estimated runtime of the job */,
  "actual_runtime" REAL /* Actual runtime of the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_jobs_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job metric */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job the metric belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was collected */,
  "cpu_utilization" REAL /* CPU utilization of the job */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the job */,
  "goodput" REAL /* Goodput of the job */,
  "packet_losses" REAL /* Packet losses of the job */,
  "network_throughput" REAL /* Network throughput of the job */,
  "gpu_utilization" REAL /* GPU utilization of the job */,
  "gpu_memory_usage" REAL /* GPU memory usage of the job */,
  "power_usage" REAL /* Power consumption of the job */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the node during the job */,
  "io_latency" REAL /* Input/output latency during the job */,
  "network_latency" REAL /* Network latency during the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_metrics_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "node_metrics_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical metric */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node the metric belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was collected */,
  "cpu_utilization" REAL /* CPU utilization of the node */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the node */,
  "nvme_from_ceph" REAL /* NVMe storage usage from Ceph */,
  "nvme_from_transfer" REAL /* NVMe storage usage from transfer */,
  "nvme_total_util" REAL /* Total NVMe storage utilization */,
  "goodput" REAL /* Goodput of the node */,
  "packet_losses" REAL /* Packet losses of the node */,
  "network_throughput" REAL /* Network throughput of the node */,
  "gpu_utilization" REAL /* GPU utilization of the node */,
  "gpu_memory_usage" REAL /* GPU memory usage of the node */,
  "power_usage" REAL /* Power consumption of the node */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the node */,
  "io_latency" REAL /* Input/output latency */,
  "network_latency" REAL /* Network latency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_node_metrics_history_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_metrics_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical metric */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job the metric belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was collected */,
  "cpu_utilization" REAL /* CPU utilization of the job */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the job */,
  "goodput" REAL /* Goodput of the job */,
  "packet_losses" REAL /* Packet losses of the job */,
  "network_throughput" REAL /* Network throughput of the job */,
  "gpu_utilization" REAL /* GPU utilization of the job */,
  "gpu_memory_usage" REAL /* GPU memory usage of the job */,
  "power_usage" REAL /* Power consumption of the job */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the node during the job */,
  "io_latency" REAL /* Input/output latency during the job */,
  "network_latency" REAL /* Network latency during the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_metrics_history_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node the alert belongs to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric that triggered the alert */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the performance metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Actual value of the performance metric */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert the notification belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the notification was sent to */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message contained in the notification */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the notification has been read */,
  "read_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)
